project.name: SimpleGenomeHub
project.description: Builds, tests, and runs the SimpleGenomeHub project.

echo.message: No local toolchain found at ${toolchain.java.home} and JAVA_HOME is not usable. Skipping bundled runtime copy.

target.name: dist
target.echo: @echo off setlocal set "SCRIPT_DIR=%~dp0" pushd "%SCRIPT_DIR%" >nul set "APP_HOME=%CD%" set "JAVA_EXE=" if exist "%APP_HOME%\runtime\bin\java.exe" set "JAVA_EXE=%APP_HOME%\runtime\bin\java.exe" if not defined JAVA_EXE if defined JAVA_HOME if exist "%JAVA_HOME%\bin\java.exe" set "JAVA_EXE=%JAVA_HOME%\bin\java.exe" if not defined JAVA_EXE set "JAVA_EXE=java" if exist "%APP_HOME%\bin\tools" set "PATH=%APP_HOME%\bin\tools;%PATH%" "%JAVA_EXE%" "-Dsimplegenomehub.app.home=%APP_HOME%" -cp "app\SimpleGenomeHub.jar;lib/*;tbtools/lib/*" simplegenomehub.gui.SimpleGenomeHubMainPanel set "EXIT_CODE=%ERRORLEVEL%" popd >nul if not "%EXIT_CODE%"=="0" ( echo. echo SimpleGenomeHub exited with code %EXIT_CODE%. echo Check runtime\ and lib\ if startup fails. pause ) exit /b %EXIT_CODE%

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)" cd "$SCRIPT_DIR" if [ -x "$SCRIPT_DIR/runtime/bin/java" ]; then JAVA_EXE="$SCRIPT_DIR/runtime/bin/java" elif [ -n "${JAVA_HOME:-}" ] && [ -x "$JAVA_HOME/bin/java" ]; then JAVA_EXE="$JAVA_HOME/bin/java" else JAVA_EXE="java" fi if [ -d "$SCRIPT_DIR/bin/tools" ]; then export PATH="$SCRIPT_DIR/bin/tools:$PATH" fi exec "$JAVA_EXE" "-Dsimplegenomehub.app.home=$SCRIPT_DIR" -cp "app/SimpleGenomeHub.jar:lib/*:tbtools/lib/*" simplegenomehub.gui.SimpleGenomeHubMainPanel

SimpleGenomeHub distribution
Contents - app/SimpleGenomeHub.jar - lib/ (general dependency jars) - tbtools/lib/TBtools_JRE1.6.jar - bin/tools/ (external executables copied from TBtools bin) - runtime/ (bundled Java 11 runtime when available) - run.bat / run.ps1 / run.sh launch scripts
Requirements - No system Java is required if runtime/ is present - If runtime/ is missing, Java 11 or newer must be available - For local builds, copy a JDK-like Java 11 toolchain into the project root toolchain/jdk/ before running ant - The application stores user configuration under the current user's home directory - The launcher sets simplegenomehub.app.home to this folder for future exe compatibility - The bundled external tools are Windows binaries, so this packaged release targets Windows
Usage on Windows - Double-click run.bat - Or from PowerShell: .\run.ps1

# SimpleGenomeHub Release Package
This directory is the current Windows release package of SimpleGenomeHub.
## Contents
```text dist/ app/ SimpleGenomeHub.jar lib/ tbtools/ lib/ TBtools_JRE1.6.jar bin/ tools/ runtime/ run.bat run.ps1 run.sh ```
## What Each Folder Is For
- `app/`: main application jar built from the local `simplegenomehub` source code - `lib/`: Java runtime dependencies used by SimpleGenomeHub - `tbtools/lib/`: TBtools-related jar dependency used in integration points - `bin/tools/`: bundled external Windows executables such as BLAST, DIAMOND, IQ-TREE and related DLL files - `runtime/`: bundled Java 11 runtime used by the launcher
## How To Run
### Windows
Double-click `run.bat`
Or start from PowerShell:
```powershell .\run.ps1 ```
## Runtime Requirement
- No system Java installation is required if `runtime/` exists - If `runtime/` is removed, Java 11 or newer must be available
## Important Notes
- This packaged release targets Windows because `bin/tools/` contains Windows executables - The launcher automatically adds `bin/tools/` to `PATH` at startup - User configuration is stored under `%USERPROFILE%\.SimpleGenomeHub\` - Species/genome project data is stored in the directory configured by the user inside the application
## Source Boundary
The main program jar in `app/SimpleGenomeHub.jar` is built from the local `src/simplegenomehub/` codebase.
This release package also contains third-party binary dependencies, including:
- TBtools-related jar dependency - external command-line tools from the TBtools toolchain - bundled Java runtime
These are packaged as runtime dependencies, not merged source code.
